summary: Cleaned up Captain Falcon and Link fighter motion-state tables by replacing local/raw flag constants and hex move-id values with named motion-flag constants and a shared FtMoveId enum. The PR moved many character-specific Fighter_MotionStateChangeFlags combinations into ftCaptain/forward.h and ftLink/forward.h, added FtMoveId to ft/forward.h, and tightened MotionState typing in ft/types.h. No PR body or review comments were present in the slice, so rationale is inferred from the diff.
what_changed: Added named Captain Falcon motion-flag constants such as ftCa_MF_SwordSwing, ftCa_MF_BatSwing, ftCa_MF_SpecialN, ftCa_MF_SpecialAirHi, and ftCa_MF_SpecialLwRebound to src/melee/ft/chara/ftCaptain/forward.h.
Removed many local ftCaptain static flag variables from ftCa_Init.c, including swing_flags_x4/x8, bat_swing_flags_x4/x8, specialn_flags_x4/x8, specialhi_flags_x4/x8, and speciallw_flags_x4/x8.
Replaced ftCaptain motion-table x4 flag entries with named ftCa_MF_* constants and replaced x8 flag/move-id constants with FtMoveId_* * (1 << 24) expressions.

Added named Link motion-flag constants to src/melee/ft/chara/ftLink/forward.h, including ftLk_MF_AttackS42, ftLk_MF_SpecialN, ftLk_MF_SpecialAirNCharge, ftLk_MF_SpecialSThrow, ftLk_MF_SpecialHi, ftLk_MF_SpecialLw, ftLk_MF_Zair, and ftLk_MF_ZairCatch.
Replaced raw Link motion-table constants such as 0x00240009, 0x00340111, 0x12000000, 0x13000000, 0x14000000, 0x15000000, and 0x01000000 with ftLk_MF_* and FtMoveId_* expressions.

Introduced a shared FtMoveId enum in src/melee/ft/forward.h, naming known move IDs such as FtMoveId_Zair, FtMoveId_SpecialN, FtMoveId_SpecialS, FtMoveId_SpecialHi, FtMoveId_SpecialLw, FtMoveId_SwordSwing, FtMoveId_BatSwing, FtMoveId_ParasolSwing, FtMoveId_HarisenSwing, FtMoveId_StarRodSwing, and FtMoveId_LipstickSwing, while preserving unknown slots as FtMoveId_UnkNN.
Updated struct MotionState in src/melee/ft/types.h so x4_flags is typed as Fighter_MotionStateChangeFlags instead of s32.
Adjusted MotionState's x8 union in ft/types.h to include a raw u32 member with a todo and a bitfield struct where move_id is typed as FtMoveId : 8.
smart_moves: Factored repeated flag combinations into base constants, especially Link's ftLk_MF_Base0/Base1/Base2/Base3 and Captain's ftCa_MF_SpecialBase0/SpecialBase1, reducing duplication while keeping exact bitwise composition visible.
Kept unknown enum values explicitly named as FtMoveId_UnkNN instead of overclaiming semantics, while naming only the recognized values such as SpecialN and item swings.
Used the existing motion table layout but made the second flag/move-id word more searchable through FtMoveId_* * (1 << 24), avoiding opaque hex constants.
Placed character-specific motion flag constants in the character forward headers so the init tables become compact and symbolically readable.
Added ATTRIBUTE_USED to static header constants, which is important for header-defined static constants that may only exist to support table initializers or matching.
decomp_lessons: Motion-state table cleanup can often proceed by first naming x4 Fighter_MotionStateChangeFlags combinations and separately identifying the x8 high-byte move ID.
When a numeric field mixes raw flags and a move-id byte, introduce an enum for the known semantic byte values but keep unknown positions as UnkNN until there is stronger evidence.
For fighter motion state tables, named constants like ftCa_MF_SpecialAirHi or ftLk_MF_ZairCatch make future diff review much safer than repeated hex values.
Typing x4_flags as Fighter_MotionStateChangeFlags documents intent and helps prevent confusing state-change flags with arbitrary signed integers.
If aggregate initialization or matching still requires raw u32 behavior, preserve a union/raw member and document the compromise with a todo rather than forcing a non-matching struct shape.
naming_conventions: Character motion flag constants use the pattern ft<CharAbbrev>_MF_<MotionName>, for example ftCa_MF_SpecialLwRebound and ftLk_MF_SpecialAirSThrowEmpty.
Reusable intra-character flag groups use Base names when the exact gameplay semantic is not known, for example ftLk_MF_Base0 through ftLk_MF_Base3 and ftCa_MF_SpecialBase0/SpecialBase1.
Move ID enum values use FtMoveId_<Name> for known meanings and FtMoveId_UnkNN for unnamed numeric slots.
Air variants are encoded in names with SpecialAir*, such as ftLk_MF_SpecialAirNFire and ftCa_MF_SpecialAirSStart.
Item swing move IDs and flags are named by item/action: SwordSwing, BatSwing, ParasolSwing, HarisenSwing, StarRodSwing, and LipstickSwing.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced x8 hex constants with FtMoveId_* * (1 << 24), matching the apparent high-byte placement of the move ID in the raw 32-bit initializer.
Retained a raw u32 member in MotionState's x8 union, with a todo to try matching without the union later, indicating the structure was shaped partly by initializer/matching constraints.
Used bitwise OR composition of FtStateChange_* flags to preserve exact constants while exposing their semantic components.
Moved static constants to headers with ATTRIBUTE_USED, a tactic to keep symbol/constant availability without triggering unused warnings or dead-stripping issues in this decompilation style.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that replace FtMoveId_UnkNN entries with named move IDs.
Search for other fighter MotionState tables still using raw x4/x8 hex constants.
Check whether the ft/types.h todo about matching MotionState without the x8 union was resolved.
Compare other character forward.h files for adoption of ft<Char>_MF_* motion flag constants.
